title: Continuity ID uniqueness is enforced across the index and records directory but not the memory directory
summary: Two distinct continuity memories currently use the identifier CM-0010: the durable replay-protection history under operations/continuity/records and the layered peer-trigger history added under operations/continuity/memory. The committed validator rejects duplicate IDs across the monolithic index and operations/continuity/records, but its executable scope does not read operations/continuity/memory. The project therefore has a namespace collision that its current integrity gate cannot observe. Neither memory is erased or declared false; the unresolved contradiction is identifier identity, not claim content.

observed: commit 88ae946a created operations/continuity/memory/CM-0010-peer-trigger-claims-are-layered.json with a different claim using the same ID.
validate-continuity-records.mjs reads only continuity-index.json and operations/continuity/records.
inferred: Any aggregate consumer that includes both storage roots cannot treat CM-0010 as an unambiguous key.
proposed: Expand the validator and effective-state aggregation contract to every sanctioned continuity storage root, then migrate one duplicate through an explicit alias or supersession record rather than deleting history.
unresolved: No canonical migration rule currently identifies which CM-0010 retains the identifier or how external references are redirected.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch both CM-0010 artifacts and verify their claim bodies differ. Inspect validate-continuity-records.mjs and reject this record if its runtime includes operations/continuity/memory or another canonical aggregator already detects the collision. Resolve the contradiction only with a committed cross-root validator plus an additive migration or alias record that preserves both historical claims.
